description: Curated read-only Windows security posture controls for Hardening Lens. Controls are Microsoft-aligned operational checks, not a verbatim vendor baseline or certification.

controls.title: Built-in Guest account is disabled
controls.description: Checks the local account identified by the well-known RID 501 rather than relying on a localized account name.
controls.rationale: An enabled guest account provides an unnecessary logon path with weak accountability.
controls.remediation: Disable the built-in Guest account through Group Policy, Intune, or local security policy and verify that no application depends on it.

controls.title: User Account Control is enabled
controls.description: Verifies that all administrators run in Admin Approval Mode.
controls.rationale: Disabling UAC removes an important privilege boundary and weakens multiple Windows security features.
controls.remediation: Set "User Account Control: Run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode" to Enabled and restart the device.

controls.title: Administrator elevation prompts require consent
controls.description: Checks that administrator elevation is not configured to occur silently.
controls.rationale: Silent elevation makes malicious or accidental privileged execution harder to detect and contain.
controls.remediation: Configure the administrator elevation prompt to "Prompt for consent for non-Windows binaries" or a stricter credential prompt.

controls.title: Built-in Administrator uses Admin Approval Mode
controls.description: Checks Admin Approval Mode for the built-in Administrator account.
controls.rationale: The built-in Administrator otherwise runs with a full token without UAC consent.
controls.remediation: Set "User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account" to Enabled.

controls.title: LSA protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks whether LSASS is configured to run as a protected process.
controls.rationale: LSA protection raises the bar for credential theft and blocks untrusted code from loading into LSASS.
controls.remediation: Enable "Configure LSASS to run as a protected process" in policy. Test authentication and security software compatibility before broad enforcement.

controls.title: WDigest credential caching is disabled
controls.description: Checks that WDigest does not retain reusable plaintext credentials in LSASS.
controls.rationale: Enabling WDigest credential caching can expose plaintext credentials to memory-access attacks.
controls.remediation: Set UseLogonCredential to 0 under the WDigest security provider policy. A missing value is treated as the secure modern default.

controls.title: Credential Guard is running
controls.description: Queries the Windows Device Guard provider and verifies that Credential Guard is active, not merely configured.
controls.rationale: Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate secrets from the normal operating system.
controls.remediation: Enable virtualization-based security and Credential Guard with the deployment mode appropriate for the device. Validate VPN, Wi-Fi, delegation, and legacy authentication dependencies first.

controls.title: Windows LAPS password backup is configured
controls.description: Evaluates the active Windows LAPS policy root and verifies that password backup targets Microsoft Entra ID or Active Directory.
controls.rationale: Unique, rotated local administrator passwords reduce lateral movement and shared-password risk.
controls.remediation: Configure Windows LAPS BackupDirectory through Intune CSP or Group Policy and validate permissions to retrieve passwords.

controls.title: Windows LAPS password age is 30 days or less
controls.description: Evaluates the effective PasswordAgeDays value from the active Windows LAPS policy root.
controls.rationale: Regular rotation limits the useful lifetime of a disclosed local administrator password.
controls.remediation: Configure PasswordAgeDays to 30 or fewer days, balancing rotation with operational recovery requirements.

controls.title: Windows LAPS AD password encryption is enabled
controls.description: When LAPS backs up to Active Directory, verifies that password encryption is enabled. Entra-backed devices are marked not applicable.
controls.rationale: AD password encryption narrows exposure of stored LAPS secrets and enables stronger authorization controls.
controls.remediation: Enable ADPasswordEncryptionEnabled for Active Directory-backed Windows LAPS after confirming the domain functional level and authorized decryptors.

controls.title: Anonymous SAM enumeration is restricted
controls.description: Checks the RestrictAnonymousSAM security option.
controls.rationale: Anonymous enumeration can disclose account information useful for reconnaissance.
controls.remediation: Set "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts" to Enabled.

controls.title: Anonymous access is restricted
controls.description: Checks the RestrictAnonymous security option.
controls.rationale: Restricting anonymous access reduces unauthenticated information disclosure and legacy null-session behavior.
controls.remediation: Set "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" to Enabled where compatible.

controls.title: Windows Firewall is enabled for every profile
controls.description: Checks Domain, Private, and Public firewall profile state.
controls.rationale: Host firewalls limit lateral movement and provide protection when network controls are absent or bypassed.
controls.remediation: Enable Windows Firewall for all profiles and deploy explicit allow rules for required management and application traffic.

controls.title: Default inbound firewall action is Block
controls.description: Checks the default inbound action for every firewall profile.
controls.rationale: A default-deny inbound posture limits unplanned exposure when no explicit rule exists.
controls.remediation: Set the default inbound action to Block for Domain, Private, and Public profiles, then maintain explicit allow rules.

controls.title: SMBv1 server component is disabled
controls.description: Checks all known SMBv1 server feature names.
controls.rationale: SMBv1 is obsolete and lacks modern security protections.
controls.remediation: Remove or disable the SMB 1.0/CIFS server component after identifying legacy dependencies.

controls.title: SMBv1 client component is disabled
controls.description: Checks all known SMBv1 client feature names.
controls.rationale: An SMBv1 client can be coerced into using an obsolete protocol against malicious or legacy servers.
controls.remediation: Remove or disable the SMB 1.0/CIFS client component after identifying legacy dependencies.

controls.title: SMB server signing is required
controls.description: Checks RequireSecuritySignature on the local SMB server.
controls.rationale: Required signing helps protect SMB traffic from tampering and relay attacks.
controls.remediation: Require SMB server signing through Group Policy or the SMB server configuration. Validate legacy clients before rollout.

controls.title: SMB client signing is required
controls.description: Checks RequireSecuritySignature on the local SMB client.
controls.rationale: Required client signing helps prevent downgrade and relay scenarios when connecting to file services.
controls.remediation: Require SMB client signing through Group Policy or SMB client configuration. Validate legacy appliances first.

controls.title: Insecure SMB guest authentication is disabled
controls.description: Checks EnableInsecureGuestLogons on the SMB client.
controls.rationale: Guest SMB authentication removes identity assurance and does not support normal signing and encryption guarantees.
controls.remediation: Disable insecure guest logons and provide authenticated access to file services.

controls.title: Remote Desktop requires Network Level Authentication
controls.description: Checks that RDP requires authentication before creating a full interactive session.
controls.rationale: NLA reduces pre-authentication attack surface and resource consumption.
controls.remediation: Enable "Require user authentication for remote connections by using Network Level Authentication."

controls.title: WinRM Basic authentication is disabled
controls.description: Checks Basic authentication on both WinRM client and service configuration.
controls.rationale: Basic authentication relies on transport protection and is unnecessary in most domain-managed environments.
controls.remediation: Disable Basic authentication for WinRM client and service. Use Kerberos, certificate authentication, or another approved mechanism.

controls.title: WinRM unencrypted traffic is disabled
controls.description: Checks AllowUnencrypted on both WinRM client and service configuration.
controls.rationale: Allowing unencrypted WinRM traffic can expose management data and credentials.
controls.remediation: Disable unencrypted WinRM traffic and use HTTPS or message-level protection provided by supported authentication protocols.

controls.title: LLMNR is disabled
controls.description: Checks the policy that disables multicast name resolution.
controls.rationale: LLMNR can enable name-resolution poisoning and credential relay when DNS resolution fails.
controls.remediation: Enable "Turn off multicast name resolution" after validating name-resolution dependencies.

controls.title: Remote Registry service is disabled
controls.description: Checks that the Remote Registry service is disabled and not running.
controls.rationale: Remote Registry expands the remote administration surface and is unnecessary for many systems.
controls.remediation: Disable the Remote Registry service unless a documented management dependency exists.

controls.title: LAN Manager authentication level refuses LM and NTLMv1
controls.description: Checks that only NTLMv2 responses are sent and LM/NTLM are refused.
controls.rationale: LM and NTLMv1 provide materially weaker authentication than NTLMv2 or Kerberos.
controls.remediation: Set "Network security: LAN Manager authentication level" to "Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM" after auditing legacy dependencies.

controls.title: LDAP server signing is required
controls.description: Checks the LDAPServerIntegrity policy on a domain controller.
controls.rationale: Required LDAP signing helps prevent modification and relay of unsigned LDAP traffic.
controls.remediation: Set "Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements" to Require signing after auditing unsigned binds.

controls.title: LDAP channel binding is enforced
controls.description: Checks the LdapEnforceChannelBinding policy.
controls.rationale: LDAP channel binding strengthens protection against credential relay over TLS.
controls.remediation: Audit compatibility, then set LdapEnforceChannelBinding to 2 (Always) through policy on domain controllers.

controls.title: LM password hashes are not stored
controls.description: Checks the NoLMHash security option.
controls.rationale: LM hashes are cryptographically weak and should not be generated for future password changes.
controls.remediation: Enable "Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change." Existing hashes disappear when passwords are changed.

controls.title: Windows LAPS backs up DSRM passwords
controls.description: For AD-backed Windows LAPS on domain controllers, verifies that DSRM password backup is enabled.
controls.rationale: Managed and rotated DSRM passwords improve recoverability without retaining shared static secrets.
controls.remediation: Enable ADBackupDSRMPassword in Windows LAPS Group Policy and validate authorized recovery procedures.

controls.title: Microsoft Defender real-time protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks the live Defender real-time protection state.
controls.rationale: Real-time protection inspects activity as files and processes are accessed.
controls.remediation: Enable real-time protection or document an approved third-party endpoint protection exception.

controls.title: Cloud-delivered protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks Defender MAPS reporting configuration.
controls.rationale: Cloud-delivered protection improves detection of new and rapidly changing threats.
controls.remediation: Enable cloud-delivered protection at the organization-approved membership level.

controls.title: Behavior monitoring is enabled
controls.description: Checks the live Defender behavior monitoring state.
controls.rationale: Behavior monitoring detects suspicious runtime activity that static signatures can miss.
controls.remediation: Enable behavior monitoring in Microsoft Defender Antivirus policy.

controls.title: Downloaded files and attachments are scanned
controls.description: Checks IOAV protection state.
controls.rationale: Scanning downloaded files and attachments reduces exposure from common delivery channels.
controls.remediation: Enable IOAV protection in Microsoft Defender Antivirus policy.

controls.title: Automatic sample submission is enabled
controls.description: Checks that Defender is allowed to submit safe or all samples automatically.
controls.rationale: Sample submission improves cloud analysis and response time for unknown files.
controls.remediation: Configure sample submission according to organizational privacy requirements; avoid Never Send unless formally accepted.

controls.title: Potentially unwanted application protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks that PUA protection is configured to block.
controls.rationale: PUA blocking reduces adware, bundlers, and software that weakens device security.
controls.remediation: Set potentially unwanted application protection to Block.

controls.title: Microsoft Defender tamper protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks the Defender-reported tamper protection state.
controls.rationale: Tamper protection helps prevent unauthorized changes to core endpoint protection settings.
controls.remediation: Enable tamper protection through the Microsoft Defender portal or supported management channel.

controls.title: Defender signatures are recent
controls.description: Calculates the age of the active antivirus signature update.
controls.rationale: Stale signatures reduce detection coverage, particularly when cloud protection is unavailable.
controls.remediation: Restore update connectivity and investigate update channel, proxy, or platform health issues.

controls.title: Defender network protection is enabled
controls.description: Checks the Defender network protection mode.
controls.rationale: Network protection blocks connections to malicious or low-reputation destinations from supported processes.
controls.remediation: Deploy network protection in audit mode first, review impact, then enable block mode.

controls.title: Core Attack Surface Reduction rules are enforced
controls.description: Evaluates a curated set of ASR rules and reports missing, disabled, audit-only, warn, and block states per rule.
controls.rationale: ASR rules disrupt common malware behaviors such as credential theft, malicious Office child processes, and ransomware activity.
controls.remediation: Pilot non-standard rules in Audit mode, tune legitimate impact, and move approved rules to Block or Warn. Avoid broad exclusions.

requiredRules.name: Block abuse of exploited vulnerable signed drivers
Block Office applications from creating child processes
Block Office applications from injecting code into other processes
Block execution of potentially obfuscated scripts
Block JavaScript or VBScript from launching downloaded executable content

controls.title: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled
controls.description: Checks known Windows PowerShell 2.0 optional feature names.
controls.rationale: PowerShell 2.0 lacks modern logging and security capabilities and can be abused for downgrade attacks.
controls.remediation: Remove the Windows PowerShell 2.0 optional feature after validating legacy script compatibility.

controls.title: PowerShell Script Block Logging is enabled
controls.description: Checks policy-backed Script Block Logging.
controls.rationale: Script Block Logging records de-obfuscated PowerShell content and provides high-value investigation telemetry.
controls.remediation: Enable "Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging." Protect and forward the PowerShell Operational log.

controls.title: PowerShell Module Logging is enabled
controls.description: Checks module logging and verifies that at least one module pattern is configured.
controls.rationale: Module logging adds pipeline and command visibility that complements Script Block Logging.
controls.remediation: Enable PowerShell Module Logging and configure approved module patterns, commonly "*" for broad visibility after sizing log collection.

controls.title: Process creation events include command lines
controls.description: Checks whether event 4688 includes process command-line data.
controls.rationale: Command-line context substantially improves investigation and detection quality for process creation events.
controls.remediation: Enable "Include command line in process creation events." Restrict Security log access because arguments can contain sensitive data.

controls.title: Credential Validation auditing captures success and failure
controls.description: Reads the effective system audit policy through the native AuditQuerySystemPolicy API.
controls.rationale: Credential validation events provide visibility into authentication attempts and failures.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Logon > Credential Validation to Success and Failure.

controls.title: Security Group Management auditing captures success
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for changes to security groups.
controls.rationale: Changes to privileged and access-control groups are high-value identity events.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Management > Security Group Management to include Success.

controls.title: User Account Management auditing captures success and failure
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for user account lifecycle and modification events.
controls.rationale: User account creation, deletion, enablement, and password changes require reliable audit visibility.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Account Management > User Account Management to Success and Failure.

controls.title: Process Creation auditing captures success
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for process creation.
controls.rationale: Process creation telemetry is foundational for Windows detection engineering and incident response.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Detailed Tracking > Process Creation to include Success.

controls.title: Logon auditing captures success and failure
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for interactive and network logons.
controls.rationale: Successful and failed logon events support authentication monitoring, investigation, and anomaly detection.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Logon/Logoff > Logon to Success and Failure.

controls.title: Special Logon auditing captures success
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for logons assigned sensitive privileges.
controls.rationale: Special logon events identify sessions receiving administrator-equivalent privileges.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Logon/Logoff > Special Logon to include Success.

controls.title: Audit Policy Change auditing captures success
controls.description: Reads effective auditing for security audit policy changes.
controls.rationale: Changes to audit policy can reduce visibility and should themselves be observable.
controls.remediation: Configure Advanced Audit Policy: Policy Change > Audit Policy Change to include Success.

controls.title: Security event log has sufficient capacity
controls.description: Checks the configured maximum Security log size.
controls.rationale: Undersized logs can overwrite evidence before it is collected or investigated.
controls.remediation: Increase the Security log maximum size and forward relevant events to centralized storage.

controls.title: System event log has sufficient capacity
controls.description: Checks the configured maximum System log size.
controls.rationale: Adequate retention supports troubleshooting and incident reconstruction.
controls.remediation: Increase the System log maximum size based on event volume and forwarding latency.

controls.title: PowerShell Operational log has sufficient capacity
controls.description: Checks the Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational log size and enabled state.
controls.rationale: PowerShell telemetry can be high volume and valuable evidence can be lost if the log is too small.
controls.remediation: Enable the PowerShell Operational log and increase its maximum size based on script activity and forwarding latency.

controls.title: Operating system volume is protected by BitLocker
controls.description: Checks BitLocker protection and encryption state for the operating system volume.
controls.rationale: Full-volume encryption protects data at rest when storage media or devices are lost or removed.
controls.remediation: Enable BitLocker using an approved protector and escrow recovery information in the organization-approved directory.

controls.title: Secure Boot is enabled
controls.description: Queries the firmware Secure Boot state.
controls.rationale: Secure Boot helps prevent untrusted boot components from loading before Windows.
controls.remediation: Enable UEFI Secure Boot after validating firmware, operating system, and virtualization support.

controls.title: Microsoft Defender SmartScreen is enforced
controls.description: Checks the machine policy that enables SmartScreen.
controls.rationale: SmartScreen helps block malicious and low-reputation downloads and sites.
controls.remediation: Enable Microsoft Defender SmartScreen through Windows security baseline or Intune policy.

controls.title: AutoRun and AutoPlay are disabled
controls.description: Checks machine policy values that disable AutoRun commands and AutoPlay behavior.
controls.rationale: Automatic execution from removable media increases malware delivery risk.
controls.remediation: Disable AutoPlay for all drives and disable AutoRun commands through Group Policy or Intune.

controls.title: Solicited Remote Assistance is disabled
controls.description: Checks whether users can request Remote Assistance.
controls.rationale: Remote Assistance adds an interactive remote-access path that should be explicitly justified.
controls.remediation: Disable "Configure Solicited Remote Assistance" unless the support model requires it; use a documented exception when enabled.
